name: Web and Scripting

tasks.title: Author Python prompt
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research this language's current ecosystem. Search for the official style guide, community-accepted linters/formatters, modern testing frameworks, current build tools, and recent paradigm shifts. Do not write from training data alone. The prompt must contain ZERO wolfcastle-specific content (no terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs). Use "prefer X" not "always use X" so agents in older codebases are not forced to contradict the prompt.

Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/python.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for Python-classified tasks. Cover: idiomatic Python style (type hints on public APIs, dataclasses/attrs for structured data, context managers, comprehensions over map/filter, pathlib over os.path), build/test tooling (pip/uv/poetry, pytest, ruff for linting and formatting, mypy/pyright for type checking), testing conventions (pytest fixtures, parametrize, conftest.py, tmp_path), and common pitfalls (mutable default arguments, late binding closures, import cycles, bare except, forgetting __init__.py in packages). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Author JavaScript prompt
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research this language's current ecosystem. Search for the official style guide, community-accepted linters/formatters, modern testing frameworks, current build tools, and recent paradigm shifts. Do not write from training data alone. The prompt must contain ZERO wolfcastle-specific content (no terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs). Use "prefer X" not "always use X" so agents in older codebases are not forced to contradict the prompt.

Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/javascript.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for JavaScript-classified tasks. Cover: idiomatic JS style (const by default, destructuring, template literals, async/await over raw promises, ESM over CJS where possible), build/test tooling (npm/pnpm/yarn, eslint, prettier, node --test or jest/vitest), testing conventions (describe/it blocks, mocking fetch/timers at boundaries, coverage), and common pitfalls (== vs ===, this binding, prototype chain surprises, callback error handling, floating promises, implicit type coercion). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Author TypeScript prompt
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research this language's current ecosystem. Search for the official style guide, community-accepted linters/formatters, modern testing frameworks, current build tools, and recent paradigm shifts. Do not write from training data alone. The prompt must contain ZERO wolfcastle-specific content (no terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs). Use "prefer X" not "always use X" so agents in older codebases are not forced to contradict the prompt.

Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/typescript.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for TypeScript-classified tasks. Cover: idiomatic TS style (strict mode always, discriminated unions over type assertions, type-only imports, utility types, branded types for domain IDs), build/test tooling (tsc, tsx/ts-node for scripts, eslint with typescript-eslint, prettier, vitest/jest with ts support), testing conventions (type-level tests where appropriate, describe/it, no any in test types), and common pitfalls (as casts hiding bugs, enum pitfalls prefer unions, declaration merging surprises, index signature gotchas, overusing generics). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Author Ruby prompt
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research this language's current ecosystem. Search for the official style guide, community-accepted linters/formatters, modern testing frameworks, current build tools, and recent paradigm shifts. Do not write from training data alone. The prompt must contain ZERO wolfcastle-specific content (no terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs). Use "prefer X" not "always use X" so agents in older codebases are not forced to contradict the prompt.

Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/ruby.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for Ruby-classified tasks. Cover: idiomatic Ruby style (duck typing, blocks/procs/lambdas, Enumerable methods, convention over configuration, freeze string literals), build/test tooling (bundler, rake, RuboCop, RSpec or Minitest), testing conventions (describe/context/it structure, let/subject, factories over fixtures with FactoryBot), and common pitfalls (monkey patching risks, method_missing abuse, gem version conflicts, require order sensitivity, mutable string default). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Author PHP prompt
tasks.body:
BEFORE WRITING: Research this language's current ecosystem. Search for the official style guide, community-accepted linters/formatters, modern testing frameworks, current build tools, and recent paradigm shifts. Do not write from training data alone. The prompt must contain ZERO wolfcastle-specific content (no terminal markers, breadcrumbs, audit gaps, deliverables, specs, ADRs). Use "prefer X" not "always use X" so agents in older codebases are not forced to contradict the prompt.

Write internal/project/templates/prompts/classes/coding/php.md (40-80 lines). REPLACES coding/default.md for PHP-classified tasks. Cover: idiomatic PHP style (strict_types declaration, type declarations on parameters and returns, readonly properties, enums, match expressions, named arguments), build/test tooling (Composer, PHPUnit, PHP-CS-Fixer or PHP_CodeSniffer with PSR-12, PHPStan/Psalm for static analysis), testing conventions (PHPUnit assertions, data providers, mocking with Prophecy or Mockery at boundaries), and common pitfalls (loose comparison table, variable variables, register_globals legacy, SQL injection in raw queries, inconsistent function parameter ordering). Use 'prefer X' phrasing. ZERO wolfcastle content.

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Web and Scripting is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify python.md, javascript.md, typescript.md, ruby.md, and php.md reflect current ecosystem tools (ruff not flake8 for Python, vitest alongside jest for JS/TS) and cover language-specific pitfalls distinct from universal.md
